editorial.headline: William Contreras Lifts Brewers Over Athletics in Extra Innings
editorial.subheadline: MIL 15, ATH 14 in a marathon that went to the 12th.
editorial.lead_angle:
William Contreras lined a sharp run-scoring single in the twelfth inning to secure a 15-14 victory for the Milwaukee Brewers over the Oakland Athletics at Las Vegas Ballpark. Abner Uribe claimed the win after working out of a late-inning jam in relief, while Chad Patrick earned the save by retiring the final three batters in order after Oakland left-hander José Suarez surrendered the go-ahead run.

Oakland jumped out to an early four-run lead behind two home runs from Tyler Soderstrom, but the tenth inning turned the game on its head. Milwaukee struck for four runs in the top of the tenth, highlighted by a Jake Bauers three-run home run. The Athletics countered immediately with four runs in the bottom of the frame, exploiting a pair of walks and a critical error to force two more innings.

Andrew Vaughn paced the Milwaukee attack with a 4-for-6 performance, driving in four runs and hitting a crucial home run to keep the visiting hitters moving. This grueling road victory keeps the Brewers within striking distance of the division lead as the summer race intensifies, while Oakland misses a critical opportunity to gain ground in their own division standings.
editorial.reason: Marathon ball. 12 innings of sustained tension makes this the day's standout.
